events.title: Dried Flower Garland Wall Art Making Workshop: Hosted by little bloomtale × KAJIHA
events.description:
KAJIHA WORKSHOP SERIES – Rituals in Dried Floral Form
Join Our NEW Dried Flower Garland Wall Art Workshop!
Through dried botanicals, we explore the passage of time, the beauty of preservation, and natural form.
This workshop offers a gentle introduction to working with dried materials. Piece by piece, you will create a wall garland that reflects nature’s rhythm and subtle movement—bringing a sense of softness and calm presence into your space.
Get ready to unleash your creativity in this fun, in-person session, where you’ll design a stunning dried flower garland that doubles as beautiful wall art. Craft cozy, nature-inspired décor using a curated selection of dried flowers and botanicals. Bring your friends or meet new ones while creating a piece you’ll love to display at home.
• Perfect for all skill levels
• Limited seats available
• Reservation required
• Booking closes 3 days in advance to allow time for material preparation
• Kid-friendly workshop: discounted pair ticket available for one adult and one child (ages 6 and up)
• All materials are included in the workshop fee
For details, please visit our website.
events.vendor: Dried Flower Garland Wall Art Making Workshop: Hosted by little bloomtale × KAJIHA

events.title: Unicorn Boy and the Slumber Party of Doom!
events.description:
In the second exciting installment of this magical adventure, our hero Unicorn Boy sets out in search of deep mystical knowledge: how to fit in at a sleep-over, how to handle everyone seeming cooler than you are, and what’s up with this whole unicorn thing? Perfect for readers of Narwhal and Jelly, Grumpy Unicorn, and InvestiGators.
Brian Reyes always felt a bit different—even before growing a unicorn horn, discovering a talking muffin, and swallowing the ruler of the Underworld to save his BFF. So when he receives a mysterious invitation for a Unicorn-Only Sleepover, Brian wonders if these fellow magical creatures might help him make sense of his increasingly odd world. Held in an enchanted castle in the clouds, not everything at this glitzy, glamorous party is as it seems, and Brian will have to fend off increasing social anxiety, ignore the negative feelings in his gut, and stay true to himself if he’s going to survive the night!
With a wave of his magic pen, Dave Roman has created a cast of charming oddballs reckoning with normal, every day problems—you know, things like heroic destinies and the fate of all magic in the universe. Join Dave and your friends at Powerhouse for a magical book launch event!
events.vendor: Unicorn Boy and the Slumber Party of Doom!

events.title: Introduction to Shochu – Tasting Class and Brewery Tour
events.description:
Are you curious about Shochu? Join us to learn the basics of this delicious and unique 500+ year old Japanese Spirit, how to differentiate between styles, and how to serve Shochu to its best advantage and use it in delicious craft cocktails. We’ll also explore the nuance of Shochu vs Sake and Soju! The course includes a guided tour of Brooklyn Kura’s sake brewery (through the lens of shochu making), a flight of 5 different shochus served in a variety of styles, and a few snacks to accompany the tasting.
What’s included:
-Shochu 101 presentation to walk you through the fundamentals of the Japanese spirit
-A guided tasting of 4 different Japanese Shochus, as well as Brooklyn Kura’s house-made Shochu
-A Handout/notes section for reference at home
-Snacks to accompany the spirit tasting
-A 10% discount for purchasing bottles at Brooklyn Kura or select bottles at Kuraichi
events.vendor: Introduction to Shochu – Tasting Class and Brewery Tour

events.description: The Isaac Brown Healing Arts & Community Center, in collaboration with Amazine Amazon & OTS, will conduct three interactive workshops, each lasting 45 to 60 minutes, on May 14. events.title: Guided Brewery Tour and Sake Tasting
events.description:
Join us for a tour of our state-of-the-art sake brewery in Industry City, Brooklyn. See first hand how we turn rice and water into delicious Brooklyn-made premium craft sake. Led by our expert brewery staff, this experience will walk you through our production facility and allow you to take in the sights, sounds and delicious smells of New York’s premier sake brewery. The tour concludes in our taproom where we will lead you a guided tasting flight of 3 kinds of Brooklyn Kura and introduce you to the nuance and variety of our award-winning sake.
Location:
Please arrive promptly in our taproom to start your tour. The location is:
34 34th Street, Inner courtyard
Industry City Building 6
Brooklyn, NY 11232
events.vendor: Guided Brewery Tour and Sake Tasting

events.title: The Comics Blueprint
events.description:
Refine your ideas or explore a new story surrounded by peers in this 2 week comics workshop led by writer and illustrator, Davis Cathcart.
In this comics workshop, students will learn how to put their story goals before their visuals in order to understand the fundamentals of visual storytelling and how the story drives imagery when the two are working in tandem. Students will execute on the ins and outs of story structure, character and telling a narrative in the cuts.
Instructor Bio: Davis Cathcart is an illustrator and writer currently residing in Ridgewood, Queens, New York. He has studied art and illustration at the Cooper Union, Maryland Institute College of Art, Rhode Island School of Design (RISD), and the School of Visual Arts. He has worked for Maira Kalman, William Wegman, and The New Yorker’s Art Department. Davis has taught art to children of all ages in public schools all over New York City. Both RISD and the Society of Illustrators have given him awards for his illustration work. He likes telling cerebral stories that do not take themselves too seriously and tries to subvert the carefree aspects of his writing with the detail of his visuals. He plans to continue to publish his work and teach.
events.vendor: The Comics Blueprint

events.description:
🪄Calling all mini magicians, fairy princesses and crafting wizards.
Carousel, a preloved kidswear box company, and Brooklyn Creative Reuse, a reused art materials shop, are teaming up on a magical wand making morning for NYC’s youngest creators.
Grab a seat and let your imagination run wild as we turn secondhand materials into extraordinary treasures. Grab lunch at one of Industry City’s delicious cafes or restaurants when your wand is finished.
Event details:
🪄For: Children ages 4+ and a parent or caregiver.
🪄Date: Drop in from 10am – 12pm on Saturday, May 16.
🪄Place: Brooklyn Creative Reuse in Industry City at 254 36th Street #C244.
🪄Cost: Pay what you wish. All proceeds go to support BRC.
🪄Tickets: Reserve your spot + wand making materials for your child at the link in BCR’s bio.
This workshop is co-hosted with Carousel! Learn more about Carousel here.

events.title: Industry City Gourmet Food and Drinks Tour
events.description:
Set against the iconic backdrop of Sunset Park’s historic waterfront, the massive, revitalized structures of Industry City form a breathtaking stage for an exclusive culinary journey. This premium food and cocktail tour is a curated exploration of taste and craft, transforming an evening into a memorable, high-end experience.
Your adventure begins with artistic bonbons at Lilac Chocolates, explores gourmet imports at Sahadi’s, and includes a refined whiskey tasting at Fort Hamilton Distillery, followed by a sophisticated sake flight. Savor additional artisanal discoveries within the awe-inspiring industrial halls.
Perfect as a distinctive date night, this tour is also fully customizable for private groups and corporate retreats. We specialize in tailoring this engaging experience for team-building and client entertainment, making it an ideal one-day corporate outing. A sophisticated mini-vacation for locals and a profound discovery for visitors.
events.vendor: Industry City Gourmet Food and Drinks Tour

events.title: MILNA MARKET
events.description:
Milna Market is coming back to Industry City on May 17th ​🥳
​​Milna Market is a curated South Asian pop-up market in NYC! We’re transforming 11,000 sq ft of space into a modern bazaar featuring 60+ South Asian brands and makers across food, beverage, fashion, jewelry, beauty, fragrance, home, lifestyle, and more.
​​​In Hindi, Milna means both “to meet” and “to find”, which is exactly what this market is all about. Join us for a full day of community, discovery, and shopping South Asian-owned businesses!
​🎟️ $4 Entry (plus tax)
Guests under 12 and over 65 receive free admission.
​Refund Policy: Tickets are non-refundable unless the event is canceled by the Organizer.
​​​🎁 The first 50 attendees will receive FREE goodies from South Asian brands 🙂
​​​🛍️ Limited amount of Milna totes available for purchase!
​​​🎉 We will have carrom, henna, and more activities!
​​​Follow us on Instagram and TikTok for updates: @milna.market

events.title: Rock and Roll Playhouse Presents Summerfeet Plays Tom Petty
events.description:
Tickets Here
PLEASE NOTE: All children must be accompanied by a parent or adult caregiver. Kids 1 and under are free. Everyone else needs a ticket. We try to keep the volume low, but we strongly encourage ear protection for everyone. Now for the fun stuff!
Industry City and Get It Right Music are proud to welcome The Rock and Roll Playhouse for a series of shows this summer in our outdoor bandshell!
This show features a return to Industry City with one of our favorite bands Summerfeet! They will be playing two sets – an opening set of Tom Petty tunes for the kiddos and their families and then a second set of straight-up Summerfeet favorites for everybody1
These shows will feature two sets!
The 1pm opening set is the traditional interactive Rock and Roll Playhouse family set with another “all ages” set for everyone’s enjoyment from approximately 230-430 pm!
Event Info
Date: May 17, 2026
Doors: 12:30pm
Rock and Roll Playhouse set:1:00-2:00pm
Rock and Roll Party for everyone: 2:30-4:30pm
Tickets: $12.50 per person plus fees
Family of Four-pack: $25 per family plus fees
Address: Industry City Bandshell Stage, Courtyard 1/2 at Industry City in Brooklyn New York between 36th and 37th street and 2nd and 3rd Avenues.
ENTER 238 36th Street, Brooklyn, NY on your GPS device
The venue is located in an open-air courtyard featuring a full concert sound stage. Fans can order food from over 17 options inside the adjoining food hall. Concert is rain or shine.
How to get here:
Take the D, N, R lines to 36th street station in Brooklyn and walk west one block to Industry City. 3 stops from Manhattan. 30 minutes from Herald Square.
From Long Island, take the LIRR to Atlantic Terminal then take the D, N or R train to 36th street.
More About the Rock and Roll Playhouse
The Rock and Roll Playhouse, a family concert series hosted at historic music venues across the country, allows kids to “move, play and sing while listening to works from the classic-rock canon” (NY Times). Performing songs created by the most iconic musicians in rock history, The Rock and Roll Playhouse band offers its core audience of families with children age ten and under games, movement, stories and an opportunity to rock out in an effort to educate children and explore their creativity. The Rock and Roll Playhouse is an early and often first introduction to a child’s lifelong journey with live music and rock and roll. See you at the show!
This concert for kids and families shares the music of the artist named above with a new generation of music lovers, but is not associated with or endorsed by the artist. All children must be accompanied by a parent or adult caregiver.
The Rock and Roll Playhouse knows that little ears are sensitive, so we turn down the volume at our shows. However, all kids experience music differently and you may find that your child is more comfortable wearing hearing protection earmuffs, such as Baby Banz, which are available for purchase here.
By attending this event, I consent to my image (with or without my voice) being included in photographs and/or film and videotape of the event, and give Brooklyn Bowl, The Rock and Roll Playhouse and their respective licensees and assigns the absolute and irrevocable right and permission to use and publish such content in any and all media, whether now known or hereafter developed.
events.vendor: Rock and Roll Playhouse Presents Summerfeet Plays Tom Petty
